metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3740
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Using an ELKS1/2 double knockout, this publication provides multiple lines of evidence that ELKS1 controls synaptic vesicle priming at excitatory synapses.

-ELKS DKO leads to a reduction in spontaneous and action potential-evoked release at excitatory synapses (Figure 2).
-ELKS DKO displays a reduction of sucrose-induced vesicle fusion, an established method for measuring vesicle priming. This effect can be rescued by reintroduction of ELKS1 (figure 7). In addition, there is a significant reduction in vesicle fusion during short action potential trains, which is in line with a defect in priming (figure 4).

13/4/2017 Pim
- Effect on priming may be synapse type related. In inhibitory synapses this effect seems absent although ELKS influences SV release via an indirect mechanism. (see ticket #333).. Experimental description: All experiments using mice were performed according to institutional guidelines at Harvard University. Conditional double knockout (cDKO) mice that remove the ELKS1α/2α proteins were generated by crossing conditional knockout mice for the Erc1 ([Liu et al., 2014] RRID:IMSR_JAX:015830) and Erc2 ([Kaeser et al., 2009] RRID:IMSR_JAX:015831) genes. ELKS1α/2α cDKO mice were maintained as double homozygote line.
